nonBlockingIssues.description: Corpus SHAs loaded but not propagated into mock reports (repo_shas empty in JSON output). Should be addressed before real tool adapters are integrated for VAL-EVAL-002 reproducibility.
truncate() helper uses byte-boundary slicing; latent UTF-8 panic risk on non-ASCII task IDs.
Ground truth overlap between disambig-003 and cross-file-003 reduces benchmark independence.
Three intent tasks use whole-file ground truth ranges, which may inflate recall metrics.
Fastify underrepresented with only 2 of 21 tasks.
Python benchmark script is 956 lines (exceeds 500-line hard limit). Acceptable for spike/benchmark scripts but AGENTS.md doesn't exempt non-Rust files.
Zero-vector fallback uses hardcoded 768-dim but Qwen3-8B produces 4096-dim vectors. Latent dimension mismatch on API failure.
Vector-only baseline limited to 500 files/repo for API cost reasons. Disclosed in report but may understate large-repo performance.
File walk comparison not perfectly apples-to-apples (different dotfile handling). Worker documented this and it doesn't affect the decision.
Cold start benchmark includes bash loop overhead, inflating both measurements slightly.
SQLite batch_size=500 vs LanceDB batch_size=1000 creates a slight asymmetry. 32× gap makes this immaterial to the decision.
Significant code duplication across 3 Python spike scripts (chunking, file listing, metrics reimplemented). Acceptable for spike code.
Bare 'except' clause in run_qwen06b.py catches KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit. Should be 'except Exception'.
Storage benchmark 768-dim vectors vs decided 4096-dim Qwen3-8B — summary doesn't flag this known limitation explicitly (documented in ADR-002).

appliedUpdates.description: Added SiliconFlow API documentation for Qwen3-0.6B fallback model to environment.md
Added spike workspace configuration guidance to environment.md

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify file size budget scope: explicitly exempt spike code (spikes/) and benchmark scripts (benchmarks/scripts/) from the 300/500-line budgets, or set separate higher limits for these directories.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: 3 features (eval-harness-setup, competitor-baselines, embedding-and-chunking-spike) had files exceeding soft/hard limits without commit message explanations. Files were 432, 956, 839, 511 lines in spike/benchmark code. Convention written as universal but seems intended for Rust source.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance for spike subdirectory Cargo configuration: spike Cargo.toml files need an empty [workspace] table to avoid being pulled into the parent workspace, and spike Cargo.lock files should be gitignored.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: storage-spike worker spent a build-fix cycle discovering the empty [workspace] requirement. language-spike worker created .gitignore for Cargo.lock. Both discoveries are repeatable for any future spike worker. AGENTS.md mentions spikes/ directory but has no Cargo configuration guidance.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note that ADR-000 (decision summary) is a consolidation document that follows a different section structure than standard ADRs. Standard ADRs use the 7-section format; summary documents are exempt.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: decision-summary reviewer flagged that ADR-000 doesn't follow the 7-section format. This is correct behavior (it's a summary, not a decision record) but the convention doesn't document the exception.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Research-worker skill: add a note distinguishing eval harness code (main source tree) from spike code (spikes/), and add guidance for data-definition/synthesis tasks where Step 4 (Run Experiments) doesn't apply. Add a standard step for creating .gitignore for spike build artifacts. Mention that spike Cargo.toml needs empty [workspace].
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: 3 workers noted skill gaps: eval-harness-setup (eval vs spike code location), benchmark-task-definitions (data-definition tasks don't fit Steps 4-5), decision-summary (synthesis tasks skip Step 4). language-spike worker had to manually create .gitignore. storage-spike worker had workspace build failure.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Benchmark-worker skill: mention that for M1 competitor baselines, real tool adapters need to be built as external scripts since the eval harness only has mock adapters at this stage.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: competitor-baselines worker built a 956-line Python script with adapter classes because the skill's Step 4 assumes tools can be run directly but the eval harness only has mock adapters. Worker spent significant effort bridging this gap.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Research-worker skill: add guidance about API timeouts for large corpora (600-900s) and suggest reducing file counts or using batched execution patterns for embedding experiments.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: embedding-and-chunking-spike worker created three separate scripts partly to work around timeout issues. Documented in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges.

rejectedObservations.observation: Parallel subagent pattern for cross-repo research should be documented in AGENTS.md
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a general agent capability, not a Vera-specific convention. Workers naturally discover Task tool parallelism. Not worth codifying.

rejectedObservations.observation: Multi-commit features lose track in handoff schema (single commitId field)
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a mission framework concern, not project guidance. The handoff schema is defined by the mission system, not AGENTS.md.

rejectedObservations.observation: ADR section naming variations (Question vs Question/Context)
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented — AGENTS.md says 'Question/Context'. Minor variations (using just 'Question') are acceptable; all required content is present. Over-specifying would be counterproductive.

rejectedObservations.observation: Storage benchmark dimension mismatch (768 vs 4096) should be called out in decision summary
rejectedObservations.reason: Already documented in ADR-002 consequences section. Adding redundant notes to ADR-000 would create maintenance burden. Workers can cross-reference ADRs.
